concepts.gloss: the indivisible meaning-bearing linguistic whole that 'bursts' into cognition

anchors.quote: अल्पे महति वा शब्दे स्फोटकालो न भिद्यते ।

concepts.gloss: the audible sound/utterance that manifests the sphoṭa
resonance; the gross acoustic vibration

anchors.quote: नादस्य क्रमजन्मत्वान्न पूर्वो न परश्च सः ।

concepts.gloss: word/language; for Bhartṛhari, the ultimate reality (śabda-tattva)

anchors.quote: अन्वाख्येयाश्च ये शब्दा ये चापि प्रतिपादकाः ॥ २४ ॥

concepts.gloss: meaning/object; that which the word conveys
speech; the faculty and levels of language

anchors.quote: अन्वाख्येयाश्च ये शब्दा ये चापि प्रतिपादकाः ॥ २४ ॥
वाक्यं वर्णपदाभ्यां च व्यतिरिक्तं न किश्चन ॥ ७२ ॥

concepts.gloss: sentence; the primary unit of meaning (akhaṇḍa-vākya)

anchors.quote: वाक्यं वर्णपदाभ्यां च व्यतिरिक्तं न किश्चन ॥ ७२ ॥

concepts.gloss: word as a grammatical unit
phoneme/letter

anchors.quote: पदे न वर्णा विद्यन्ते वर्णेष्ववयवा न च ।

concepts.gloss: universal/class; the śabda-jāti view of meaning
apparent transformation of the one word-principle into the manifold
the intuitive flash by which sentence-meaning is grasped

anchors.quote: सत्यासत्यौ तु यो भावौ प्रतिभावं व्यवस्थितौ ।

concepts.gloss: time; kāla-śakti orders action and manifestation

anchors.quote: क्रमो हि धर्मः कालस्य तेन वाक्यं न विद्यते ॥ ५० ॥

concepts.gloss: power/capacity (e.g. of a word to signify)

anchors.quote: ग्राह्यत्वं ग्राहकत्वं च द्वे शक्ती तेजसो यथा ।

concepts.gloss: the absolute; identified with śabda-tattva
relation (esp. word–meaning relation)

anchors.quote: धर्मे ये प्रत्यये चाङ्गं संबन्धाः साध्वसाधुषु ॥ २५ ॥
एवं च सति सम्बन्धः शब्दस्यार्थेन हीयते ॥ १६ ॥

concepts.gloss: action; verb-meaning, primary in the verb-first ontology
means/instrument; kāraka participants of an action

anchors.quote: नियतास्तु प्रयोगा ये नियतं यच्च साधनम् ।

concepts.gloss: cognition/intellect in which meaning appears

anchors.quote: यथा प्रयोक्तुः प्राग् बुद्धिः शब्देष्वेव प्रवर्तते ।

concepts.gloss: limiting adjunct/condition

curated_relations.note: the sphoṭa is revealed by the dhvani
the one Word unfolds by vivarta into the manifold
an action is structured by its kāraka means
